exemplar.title: Investigating The Influence Of Wire Diameter On Fundamental Frequency And Its Application In Determining The Density Of Nichrome.

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating The Influence Of Wire Diameter On Fundamental Frequency And Its Application In Determining The Density Of Nichrome.

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables (raw and processed) are clearly formatted with appropriate headings, units and systematic uncertainties
Graphs include properly labeled axes, error bars and clear slope-range shading
weaknesses: Treatment of tension and length uncertainties is superficial; choice of Â±0.1 Hz lacks quantitative justification
Inconsistent notation and significant figures in uncertainty propagation and slope reporting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although communication of data is very clear (6-band), two strands (uncertainty consideration and processing accuracy) show significant omissions or inconsistencies (4-band). The overall profile fits mid-band performance, meriting a score of 4.

strengths: Research question framed in a highly specific engineeringâmusic context, demonstrating clear practical relevance
Procedure described with labelled diagram, equipment list and controlâvariable table enabling reproduction
weaknesses: Minor ambiguity in the count and listing of wire diameters (duplicate 0.27 mm entry)
Coupling of the signal generator and oscilloscope lacks full detail for exact replication
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands met 5â6-band descriptors (context and data-collection methodology), while the third (methodological considerations) sat firmly at the 3â4 band. The balanced profile warrants a high mid-band score of 5, reflecting strong contextualisation and reproducibility with only minor omissions.

strengths: Conclusion restates the linear relationship and references the strong statistical fit (RÂ²â0.99)
Quantitative comparison with literature density and calculation of percentage error
weaknesses: Misattribution of systematic error (evaporation of reactants) is irrelevant to a vibration experiment
Discussion lacks depth on the statistical meaning of RÂ² and its deviation from unity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands nominally met the 5â6-band descriptors, but the conceptual error in error attribution and limited discussion of fit statistics weaken the scientific consistency. A mid-band score of 4 accurately reflects these shortcomings.

strengths: Identifies three specific methodological weaknesses with clear evidence and impact on density calculation
Proposes realistic, context-specific improvements and explains how each would mitigate errors
weaknesses: Suggested quantification of wire-diameter increments is lacking; exact step sizes are not specified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both evaluation strands show high-level analysis and justification (6-band), but a minor omission in quantifying improvement recommendations prevents a perfect score. A high mid-band score of 5 reflects the thorough but not entirely exhaustive evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly stated, specifies both the independent (wire diameter) and dependent (fundamental frequency / derived density) variables, and is embedded in a concrete engineering-and-music context.  The introduction gives concise, discipline-specific justification (bridge resonance, stringed-instrument design) showing the practical significance of the investigation.  This specificity meets the descriptor for a âspecific and appropriate context,â placing the work firmly in the 5-6 band.

Uncertainties are included for every measured and derived quantity, error bars are plotted and slope uncertainty is estimated via max/min gradients.  Nonetheless, some significant omissions/misapplications persist: tension and length uncertainties are treated superficially, units occasionally drop (e.g. HzÂ² written as "Hz2"), and the choice of Â±0.1 Hz ignores oscilloscope resolution rationale.  Hence consideration is appropriate but not fully rigorousâbest represented by the 3-4 band, top score 4.

Methodological considerations are presented through preliminary trials, a control-variable table and a discussion of tension choice.  The student describes why each variable is kept constant and how uncertainties arise, showing awareness of relevance and sufficiency.  However, explanations remain qualitative (e.g. no quantitative justification for number of diameters or trials), so the strand is best described as "described" rather than fully "explained," aligning with the 3-4 band; the stronger edge of that band (4) is appropriate.

Three specific methodological weaknesses are identified, each accompanied by evidence, direction and magnitude of effect on the calculated density.  The student explains how rattling or wire deformation steepens the slope and thus lowers Ï, demonstrating clear understanding of relative impact.  This meets the descriptor for the highest band.

The procedural section supplies a labelled diagram, numbered steps, equipment list, control-variable table and measurement uncertainties.  A competent experimenter could reproduce the set-up without seeking extra information.  Minor ambiguities remain (e.g. the exact way the generator is coupled to the wire, calibration of the oscilloscope), so full perfection is not reached, but the description is sufficiently complete for replication, fitting comfortably in the 5-6 band; a score of 5 reflects these small gaps.

Data processing covers averaging, conversion to area, inverse area, propagation, linear plotting, slope extraction and density calculation.  The mathematics is largely correct and consistent with theory, yet a few inaccuracies creep in (mis-typed variable 1/4 instead of 1/A, density rounding, unit scaling confusion).  These constitute âsome significant inaccuracies,â so band 3-4 is justified; score 4 acknowledges the otherwise sound execution.

For every weakness, the student proposes realistic, context-specific improvements (e.g. uniform diameter range, smoothing wires, altering generator placement) and justifies how each would mitigate the identified issue.  Suggestions are not merely stated but explained, satisfying the top-band criteria.

Raw- and processed-data tables are neatly formatted with correct headings, units and uncertainties; graphs use proper axes labels, error bars and gradient ranges.  Calculations are set out step-by-step.  Both clarity (reader can follow logic) and precision (units, significant figures, propagation methods) are maintained, satisfying the top-band descriptor.

The experimental density is compared quantitatively with a literature value, and percentage error is calculated.  Plausible scientific reasons (systematic error sources, material properties) are offered for the discrepancy.  This constitutes a relevant, well-supported comparison to accepted scientific context, aligning with the 5-6 band; minor depth issues warrant a mid-band score of 5.

The student states a clear conclusion about the linear relationship and derives a numerical density, explicitly referencing the graphed data and statistical fit (RÂ²â0.99).  The reasoning ties directly to processed results, though minor computational weaknesses reduce absolute certainty.  Overall, the conclusion is "justified and fully consistent" with the analysis, meriting the 5-6 band; a score of 5 reflects the small calculation caveats.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively situates the research question within both engineering and musical contexts, demonstrating specific and appropriate relevance to real-world applications.
The list of wire diameters repeats 0.27 mm and mentions nine values while stating eight. Clarify the distinct diameters and ensure consistency between quantity and list.
The derivation jumps from the wave equation to $$v=f(2L)$$ without an explicit intermediate step. Include the rearrangement to $$f=\tfrac{v}{2L}$$ for clarity.
The preliminary investigation section clearly identifies and addresses setup challenges, showing thoughtful methodological consideration.
The description of the independent variable lists nine measurements but states eight wires. Specify the exact count and remove duplicate diameters to avoid ambiguity.
Itâs unclear how the signal generator is coupled to the wire and oscilloscope. Include a precise description or diagram of the connections to ensure reproducibility.
The control-variable table is comprehensive with clear justifications, enabling replication of experimental conditions.
Room temperature control is stated but lacks quantified range or monitoring method. Specify the actual temperature and its allowed fluctuation.
Raw data table headings use notation âmÂ² 10-6â which is ambiguous. Adopt standard scientific notation (e.g. Ã10â»â¶ mÂ²) to improve clarity.
The rationale for choosing Â±0.1 Hz uncertainty is brief. Provide a quantitative justification based on oscilloscope resolution and visual detection limits.
In the exemplar calculations, the number of significant figures and decimal places are inconsistent. Align with uncertainty rules and report correct precision.
The processed data table includes uncertainties systematically, supporting clear data communication and precision.
The final data table presents inverse areas and squared frequencies clearly, facilitating straightforward plotting.
Some entries in the final table use differing decimal precision. Ensure significant figures match uncertaintyâfor instance, represent 1.61 consistently.
The scatter plot with error bars clearly illustrates the linear trend, demonstrating both clarity and precision in data presentation.
The reported slope â(7Â±1)Ã10â»âµâ contradicts the more precise value of 73.42Ã10â»â¶. Maintain consistent significant figures and unit notation.
The method for estimating slope uncertainty via max/min gradients is described briefly. Include explicit values and a clear step-by-step calculation.
The density calculation omits the unit mâ»Â³ in the final expression. Append correct SI units and specify any rounding applied.
The uncertainty propagation for density could include a brief justification for approximating ÎL/L and Îm/m. Clarify assumptions in the fractional addition method.
The conclusion concisely restates the linear relationship and links back to theoretical expectations, fully consistent with the analysis.
The comparison cites a 16.7 % error but attributes discrepancy to evaporation of reactantsâirrelevant to a wire vibration experiment. Correct systematic error sources.
Including the RÂ² value strengthens the conclusion. Consider discussing its calculation method and its marginal deviation from 1.
The evaluation acknowledges methodological strengths but could better integrate these with their impact on data quality, linking to specific weaknesses identified.
The systematic error table clearly specifies source, evidence, direction, magnitude and improvement, demonstrating high-level evaluation.
The random error summary effectively prioritizes sources by relative impact and suggests concrete improvements, showing thorough evaluation.
The suggestion to use more regular increments of wire thickness is insightful. Quantify recommended step sizes to avoid overemphasis of outliers.
The final note on using strings is interrupted mid-sentence. Complete the rationale to clarify how strings improve flexibility and tension control.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
